canonical_title: Court gives final approval to Anthropic's $1.5 billion copyright settlement
organizations: U.S. District Court for the Northern District of California
treatment: Treat as a finalized legal settlement concerning pirated source copies used in the training-data pipeline, distinct from the court's fair-use holding on model training. Reinclude only for appeal, payment/distribution changes, separate opt-out litigation, or a material change to Anthropic's data-acquisition policy.
known_facts: Reuters reported on 2026-07-20 at 20:49 UTC that a federal judge granted final approval to Anthropic's $1.5 billion class-action copyright settlement.
The underlying ruling maintained that training on copyrighted books was fair use, while finding infringement in Anthropic's storage of more than seven million pirated books in a central library.
More than 91% of covered authors and publishers had claimed compensation at the time of approval.
Some authors and publishers opted out and continue separate lawsuits.
reinclude_only_if: The settlement is appealed, modified, delayed, or materially changes distribution terms.
Separate opt-out litigation produces a major ruling or additional liability.
Anthropic announces a material training-data acquisition, provenance, or licensing policy change.
Another court adopts or rejects the distinction between fair-use training and unlawful acquisition or storage of source material.
notes: Selected because final judicial approval occurred inside the current delta window and converts a previously negotiated settlement into a legally operative resolution.
